text:
In this paper, we develop a mathematically tractable and general framework to analyze data traffic offloading in M-RAT K-tiers heterogeneous networks (HetNets). While the offloading of data traffic from cellular networks to smaller wireless networks such as WiFi is a key strategy to deal with increasing mobile data traffic, its impact on the overall network performance and rate distribution is not yet well understood. Using stochastic geometry, we model APs and mobile users locations by independent Poisson point processes (PPPs) with different transmit powers, path loss exponents, intensities and available bandwidths for different network tiers.

We apply a flexible weighted association scheme and obtain the distribution of the SINR and rates at all points of the network. We characterise the association regions as a generalized multiplicatively weighted Poisson Voronoi tessellation, and approximate accurately the cell loads. This analysis reveals important design insights: i) that the optimal power offloading levels for maximizing the coverage of the SINR and the rates are different, and depend strongly on different sets of parameters; ii) that the optimal power for maximizing the coverage of the rate has a large dependency on the ratio of the available bandwidth to the user's quality of service (QoS) requirement; iii) that the coverage of the rates increases with the increased density of the small cell infrastructure. Our results are validated by simulations with measured data from real-world cellular and WiFi networks, facilitating the design of efficient and optimal heterogeneous wireless networks.
